text:
@TheDoremi40 

Oh, absolutely...I'm not trying to say that the general message of this is bad, given its audience...I'm saying it's a bad policy to have as a general principle. ESPECIALLY when three of the "bad" ones, namely Alcohol, Tobacco, and Caffeine, are legal and all but encouraged in our society, for largely economic reasons. Children certainly shouldn't be taking any thing...but I think that having cannabis be the demon was in poor taste 0 Granted, this was "Gate-Way Drug" Era, so...
author: @Mithradates.Eupator

text: Well, what else are they supposed to do when they get together?
author: @wilderulz

text: @Tethyrin I see. Well, on the point of the movie, a lot of kids wouldn't  be able to discern a harmful substance from a good substance. But, unfortunately, later on, they'd still be bombarded with how all substances are bad. So, what I mean is, this movie's moral was meant to stop kids since they wouldn't know which substances are good or bad. Great. But later on, true, all we see are the same morals over and over again even though at a high-school age we should be able to tell for ourselves.
author: @TheDoremi40

text: @TheDoremi40 That's...likely true. I come at it from a historical perspective more than anything. Cultures that used some substances (cannabis, ergot-derived LSA, Psilocybin Mushrooms, Ayhuasca, etc.) have generally outlived cultures made excessive use of opiates, and other, more dangerous, substances. All of the cultures that have a reputation of high creativity and innovation (The Grecco-Romans, India, China, the Mezo-Americans, etc) have had intense relationships to these positive substances.
author: @Mithradates.Eupator

text: @Tethyrin That's a good point. Not quite being a substance user myself, I can't tell you which ones are bad, which ones are good, or whether or not all of them are bad/good. Of course, I can't be entirely sure if you're a substance user yourself, but I'm guessing that you would know more about specific substances than I do.
author: @TheDoremi40

text:
@TheDoremi40 

Indeed. But my point is that it's not inherently worse because the addiction is "drugs" instead of "television" or "shopping". Yes, some substances are very harmful...but not all are. I just feel that the message presented here that "drugs are bad" is an EXTREME oversimplification...and an irresponsible one.
